client_context.facility_line: Zurich Cheese Works & Bern Yogurt Plant, Swiss Confederation
client_context.jurisdiction_label: EU & Swiss Sustainability Frameworks for Dairy Manufacturers

executive_summary:
February 2026 sees Alpine Dairy AG facing its most concentrated compliance action window of the year. The CSRD FY2024 reporting deadline of 30 June 2026 is now four months away, and the company's sustainability disclosure team has engaged external assurance provider PwC Basel for limited assurance. The double materiality assessment was completed internally but the external ESRS S1 (own workforce) gap analysis has flagged two issues: gender pay equity data disaggregation below plant level, and the absence of a supplier social audit programme covering the company's 38 contracted Swiss dairy farms.

The DE-VerpackDG-2026 draft amendment to Germany's Verpackungsdurchführungsgesetz was published for parliamentary consultation in February 2026, introducing a differentiated EPR fee structure that penalises dairy packaging with multilayer barrier films containing PFAS coating. Alpine Dairy's export carton lines to Germany use an EVOH-barrier multilayer format that likely falls within the high-fee tier under Article 12c of the draft. The amendment is expected to pass before the Bundesrat summer recess.

The European Commission published a draft delegated act under PPWR specifying recyclability scoring criteria for food-contact multilayer packaging in February 2026. The draft classifies EVOH-containing aseptic dairy cartons as 'recyclability class C' — a classification that triggers extended producer responsibility surcharges from 2028. Alpine Dairy's head of sustainability has escalated this to the board, recommending acceleration of the packaging redesign programme already budgeted for 2027.

sections.heading: EU Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation — Delegated Act Draft
paragraphs: The Commission published a draft delegated act under PPWR Article 6(5) in February 2026 establishing a four-tier recyclability classification system for food-contact packaging. Dairy sector multilayer aseptic cartons containing EVOH or aluminium barrier layers are classified as 'Class C' — non-recyclable by design — unless manufacturers demonstrate compliance with a certified recyclability protocol by 2028. Class C packaging will be subject to EPR surcharges of EUR 0.08–0.12 per unit from 2028, rising to EUR 0.20 per unit from 2030 if redesign is not achieved.
sections.callout: Engage packaging R&D team and carton supplier (SIG Combibloc or Tetra Pak) to assess mono-material or reduced-barrier alternatives; target Class B reclassification by 2027.

sections.heading: Verpackungsdurchführungsgesetz Amendment
paragraphs: Germany's draft VerpackDG-2026 amendment introduces Article 12c, a differentiated EPR fee schedule penalising packaging containing PFAS coatings and non-recyclable barrier films. The proposed fee differential is EUR 0.05–0.18 per kilogram of packaging placed on the German market, with the higher tier applying to multilayer formats with EVOH or aluminium barriers. Parliamentary consultation closes 31 March 2026; Bundesrat vote expected May 2026.
sections.callout: Submit industry comments via BVE (Bundesvereinigung der Deutschen Ernährungsindustrie) consultation channel by 28 March 2026; quantify annual EPR fee exposure under proposed Article 12c.

sections.heading: Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive — FY2024 Reporting
paragraphs: ESMA published interpretive guidance in February 2026 clarifying that ESRS S1 Own Workforce disclosures must include gender pay gap data disaggregated by job category and facility for entities with more than two manufacturing sites. Alpine Dairy AG's EU entities are required to report disaggregated pay equity metrics across Zurich Cheese Works and Bern Yogurt Plant employee categories. The absence of a supplier social audit programme for contracted dairy farms is flagged as a potential ESRS S2 (Workers in the Value Chain) gap.
sections.callout: Implement gender pay gap disaggregation by job category and facility; initiate pilot social audit programme for top 10 contracted dairy farms by 30 April 2026.

sections.heading: Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive — AT Transposition Progress
paragraphs: Austria's Nationalrat approved the first reading of the CSDDD transposition bill in February 2026. The Austrian bill introduces a private right of action for value chain workers, allowing Austrian trade unions to bring civil claims against companies for CSDDD non-compliance on behalf of affected workers. This significantly raises the litigation risk profile for Alpine Dairy's Austrian subsidiary compared to the German transposition approach.
sections.callout: Review Austrian subsidiary supplier contracts to ensure CSDDD-compliant contractual clauses are in place; assess trade union engagement strategy.

rows: Germany's draft VerpackDG-2026 amendment introduces Article 12c, a differentiated EPR fee schedule penalising packaging containing PFAS coatings and non-recyclable barrier films. The proposed fee differential is EUR 0.05–0.18 per kilogram of packaging placed on the German market, with the higher tier applying to multilayer formats with EVOH or aluminium barriers. Parliamentary consultation closes 31 March 2026; Bundesrat vote expected May 2026.
Submit industry comments via BVE (Bundesvereinigung der Deutschen Ernährungsindustrie) consultation channel by 28 March 2026; quantify annual EPR fee exposure under proposed Article 12c.

ESMA published interpretive guidance in February 2026 clarifying that ESRS S1 Own Workforce disclosures must include gender pay gap data disaggregated by job category and facility for entities with more than two manufacturing sites. Alpine Dairy AG's EU entities are required to report disaggregated pay equity metrics across Zurich Cheese Works and Bern Yogurt Plant employee categories. The absence of a supplier social audit programme for contracted dairy farms is flagged as a potential ESRS S2 (Workers in the Value Chain) gap.
Implement gender pay gap disaggregation by job category and facility; initiate pilot social audit programme for top 10 contracted dairy farms by 30 April 2026.

references.citation: Regulation (EU) 2025/40 on packaging and packaging waste

content_updates.title: EU Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation — Delegated Act Draft
content_updates.summary: The Commission published a draft delegated act under PPWR Article 6(5) in February 2026 establishing a four-tier recyclability classification system for food-contact packaging. Dairy sector multilayer aseptic cartons containing EVOH or aluminium barrier layers are classified as 'Class C' — non-recyclable by design — unless manufacturers demonstrate compliance with a certified recyclability protocol by 2028. Class C packaging will be subject to EPR surcharges of EUR 0.08–0.12 per unit from 2028, rising to EUR 0.20 per unit from 2030 if redesign is not achieved.
content_updates.action_required: Engage packaging R&D team and carton supplier (SIG Combibloc or Tetra Pak) to assess mono-material or reduced-barrier alternatives; target Class B reclassification by 2027.

changes.description: Draft delegated act classifies EVOH multilayer dairy cartons as Class C non-recyclable

content_updates.title: Verpackungsdurchführungsgesetz Amendment
content_updates.summary: Germany's draft VerpackDG-2026 amendment introduces Article 12c, a differentiated EPR fee schedule penalising packaging containing PFAS coatings and non-recyclable barrier films. The proposed fee differential is EUR 0.05–0.18 per kilogram of packaging placed on the German market, with the higher tier applying to multilayer formats with EVOH or aluminium barriers. Parliamentary consultation closes 31 March 2026; Bundesrat vote expected May 2026.

changes.description: Article 12c EPR fee differentiation for PFAS/non-recyclable packaging added to draft

Alpine Dairy AG's EU entities are required to report disaggregated pay equity metrics across Zurich Cheese Works and Bern Yogurt Plant employee categories. The absence of a supplier social audit programme for contracted dairy farms is flagged as a potential ESRS S2 (Workers in the Value Chain) gap.

content_updates.title: Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive — AT Transposition Progress
content_updates.summary: Austria's Nationalrat approved the first reading of the CSDDD transposition bill in February 2026. The Austrian bill introduces a private right of action for value chain workers, allowing Austrian trade unions to bring civil claims against companies for CSDDD non-compliance on behalf of affected workers. This significantly raises the litigation risk profile for Alpine Dairy's Austrian subsidiary compared to the German transposition approach.
content_updates.action_required: Review Austrian subsidiary supplier contracts to ensure CSDDD-compliant contractual clauses are in place; assess trade union engagement strategy.

changes.description: Austrian Nationalrat approved first reading of CSDDD transposition bill

critical_actions.title: EU Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation
critical_actions.action: EVOH multilayer cartons likely Class C — accelerate redesign programme to avoid 2028 EPR surcharges
